name: Tina Smith
subject: Happy Pride!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
I promise to never stop working for equal rights and opportunity for all.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190623_em&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Friend,

Today, I marched in the Twin Cities Pride Parade -- a wonderful celebration of the LGBTQ+ community and how far we've come in the fight for equality. [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190623_em&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") I'm proud to represent a state where all are welcome and where we continue to fight the injustices that exist for our LGBTQ+ family. Everyone should be able to be who we were born to be without fear of facing discrimination or violence.

But this is a challenging time for members of the LGBTQ+ community across the country. Last month, Donald Trump announced plans to roll back nondiscrimination protections for transgender people. Prior to that, the Trump administration issued a rule allowing doctors to refuse patients on religious grounds -- thereby allowing them to deny health care to our LGBTQ+ friends and neighbors.

And in Congress, though the U.S. House passed the Equality Act, which prohibits discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity, Mitch McConnell refuses to hold a vote in the Senate.

**Friend, it 's more important than ever that we raise our voices, lift each other up, and build an America where everyone -- regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity -- has an equal opportunity to thrive.**

I promise to never stop working for equal rights and opportunity for all. I'm grateful to have your support in this fight.

Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. Make sure she has the resources to fight back.** [CONTRIBUTE](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190623_em-biobox&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto")

name: Governor Ray Mabus
subject: Jim Hood is the right choice
body:
|

I'm reaching out because I want you to know that in this Governor's race there's only one person for the job, and I'm proud to be supporting Jim Hood.
Friend,

I'm reaching out because I want you to know that in this Governor's race there's only one person for the job, and I'm proud to be supporting Jim Hood.

Over the past 16 years, Jim has repeatedly made the people of Mississippi proud as our Attorney General. He's protected our land from polluters, held big corporations accountable, and defended our children against online predators.

I know that as Governor, he will continue his track record of fighting for Mississippi families. But in order to succeed, **Jim is depending on your support.**

I know firsthand that putting together a winning gubernatorial campaign is no easy feat. Jim, his staff, and an army of volunteers are working overtime to reach voters in every corner of the state.

**[Your donation today will ensure that Jim has the resources to run a winning campaign.](https://act.myngp.com/el/S87vNAVa0QEf0c_s0lU3xB3n_E5C-IDFWybYwP1QWQE=/7nZM5X5zgDA_QfxSgB-1M9xONccgFJP9_AU4DJJX8SI=?refcode=20190624&amount=25)** He is counting on supporters like you to help spread his message and combat the flow of dark money and attack ads in this race.

I'm supporting Jim because throughout his career serving Mississippi, he has proven that he will do what is right for our state.

**[Please contribute whatever you can today, and join me in supporting Jim Hood for Governor.](https://act.myngp.com/el/S87vNAVa0QEf0c_s0lU3xB3n_E5C-IDFWybYwP1QWQE=/7nZM5X5zgDA_QfxSgB-1M9xONccgFJP9_AU4DJJX8SI=?refcode=20190624&amount=25)**

Ray Mabus

body:
President Trump does not feel tied down by the unsuccessful formulas of the past. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=516659fad5&e=3ee4985eea)

| | |
# **President Trump's Middle East Plan is a Refreshing Change**

“The Trump administration is no less committed to peace than its predecessors. The key difference is that Trump does not feel tied down by the unsuccessful formulas of the past, and he and his team are willing to openly challenge that conventional thinking,” Hudson Institute Senior Fellow Jon Lerner writes in CNN.

“The goal of the Trump plan is to end this decades-long conflict and create conditions for a better life for Palestinians and Israelis. The plan will begin to be rolled out at this week's conference in Bahrain,” Lerner writes. “Economic conditions are quite poor in the West Bank, and among the world's worst in Gaza . . . The Trump plan contains specific and realistic projects that could double Palestinian GDP, create a million new jobs in Gaza and the West Bank, and cut poverty in half.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0242129daf&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The White House has unveiled the economic aspects of its Middle East peace plan,” Prof. Emeritus Alan Dershowitz of Harvard Law School writes in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e1ba279e7&e=3ee4985eea). “The bottom line is that if Palestinian leaders were to accept — or at least sit down and negotiate about —the proposed peace plan, they could quickly improve the quality of life of their people. This could lead to a two-state solution that would be a win-win for all sides.”
---
|
---
| “Critics would rather bash Trump than acknowledge Obama’s naïve strategy of Iranian appeasement was a failure,” Middle East expert Tom Basile writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a77b39aafb&e=3ee4985eea). “Democrats and Republicans in Congress need to acknowledge that the Obama approach to Iran emboldened and enriched an enemy never willing to be an honest broker for peace. President Trump is trying to get the West back in a position to effectively isolate Iran and counter the Mullahs aggression before engaging in any form of military conflict.”
---
|
---
| “Concerns about immigration, and its impact on crime and taxes, have soared to the highest level ever, according to a new survey. Gallup said that more Americans than ever before cited immigration as their top concern in the latest poll,” Paul Bedard reports in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e2b32aa2c2&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “What do Democrats want to do about illegal immigration? Will they ever tell us? Or will they keep emoting about how deeply they care about the issue without ever offering a plan?” Karol Markowicz writes in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8c27cb8702&e=3ee4985eea). “Democrats know what they oppose: any proposal from President Trump . . . They want the emotional validation of being the ‘good guys’ without having to actually lay out their positions on what to do about tens of thousands of people entering the homeland illegally.”
---
|

We need to keep growing our grassroots movement. Our campaign is facing a powerfully important test.

**On Sunday, we 'll hit the next FEC fundraising deadline. Whether or not we hit our goal will make a real difference in the types of investments we can make going forward.**

The pressure is on. But here's what you're _not_ going to see me do:

I still won't host any private events where you can only get in and meet me if you can write a big check.

I still won't take a dime from any federally registered lobbyist, corporate PAC, or, shoot, any PAC of any kind.

And I still won't suck up to anyone who might dump buckets of cash into a Super PAC for me.

**The rich and powerful already have too much influence on our government. We 're in this fight to change all that, and it starts with how we fund our campaign.

There's only one way we're raising money in this campaign, and it's with grassroots donors. subject: what are GOP lawmakers saying about me?

body:
I often ask you to invest in our 2020 re-election efforts because I know my seat is a top target, and I'll need tremendous resources to communicate with voters across our large district.
| | Dear Peter,

I often ask you to invest in our 2020 re-election efforts because I know my seat is a top target, and I'll need tremendous resources to communicate with voters across our large district.

**But that 's the mechanics of why I ask; the motivation behind my request is that I believe in the work I'm doing in Congress. I believe in the mission of service. And I believe my colleagues and I are having a positive impact.**

My colleagues on the other side of the aisle are noticing my hard work, too! In Thursday's issue of _The Hill_ , a senior GOP lawmaker acknowledged that not only did Democrats win the majority because of elections like mine, but that I am doing a good job.

The lawmaker, who spoke anonymously, said, "[The Democrats] have really good majority-makers -- [Reps.] Abigail Spanberger, Dean Phillips, Max Rose. They've got some good members that know what they're doing."1 **[I'll take the compliment and ask that you again consider making an investment in my reelection campaign so that we can hold the majority and I can keep being a "good member who knows what she's doing."](https://act.myngp.com/el/8pWDV_tEWpgcWiDSqpEwGjgMNEvWgsEaFX4yvEmHWrE=/3PrTq9ULOyhkkFBnWMWF7XGJ8OJElRGspCweBbDTE2w=?refcode=em_fr_2019.06.24_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)** | ********[CONTRIBUTE »](https://act.myngp.com/el/8pWDV_tEWpgcWiDSqpEwGjgMNEvWgsEaFX4yvEmHWrE=/3PrTq9ULOyhkkFBnWMWF7XGJ8OJElRGspCweBbDTE2w=?refcode=em_fr_2019.06.24_b1_as_all&amount=25&amounts=10,25,50,100,250,1000,2800)********

This weekend, I was in South Carolina standing as a proud partner with Planned Parenthood.

Right now, women's reproductive rights are under assault all over the United States.

So I want to be absolutely clear: women across the country should have the right to safe, legal, and accessible abortion, birth control, cancer screenings, and health care.

[Will you take action today and stand with Planned Parenthood too?](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba238/134673f2/1ed7571/6996d1b9/4264993685/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/Defend-Planned-Parenthood?source=EM_PET_B1_062419_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

[STAND WITH PLANNED PARENTHOOD ->](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cba238/134673f2/1ed7571/6996d1b9/4264993685/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/s/Defend-Planned-Parenthood?source=EM_PET_B1_062419_AC_ND&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")
---
As President, I will do everything I possibly can do to ensure that Planned Parenthood is protected and funded. Because health care decisions should be in the hands of women and their families. _Period._
---
Forward together,

Michael

We know we can win, but winning takes money. Chip in today and help us tell Georgia voters exactly the kind of senator they have in David Perdue. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tt-eoq?refcode=em062419-a&noskip=true]

body:

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7769120/73951090/368741054?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTJhZDlmOC1jZTk2LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_XD_RSO-i3MoYHk5mzZ4FbmPdbV40uDKMzAXCUquiG0=)

| | | | Peter,

**June is National Oceans Month!**

The oceans play a key role in our environment — but as carbon emissions grow and plastic waste accumulates, they’re becoming polluted, marine wildlife are being affected and it’s impacting our oceans’ balance.

From the Great Lakes to our oceans, I have been leading the effort to support and preserve our bodies of water. The cost of inaction is just too high.

[**Peter, we must take action to prevent further pollution and damage. Sign our petition now and tell Congress to protect our oceans.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7769122/73951094/2098101291?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTJhZDlmOC1jZTk2LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_XD_RSO-i3MoYHk5mzZ4FbmPdbV40uDKMzAXCUquiG0=)

I have worked hard in Congress to find effective solutions to deal with climate threats. I even helped shut down a Sterigenics plant in our district that was emitting toxic chemicals.

But the GOP has been silent on this issue for far too long. Climate change is the single biggest threat to our health and well-being — we cannot delay action any longer.

[**Add your name next to mine and urge Congress to take immediate action and protect our oceans!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/7769124/73951096/2098101291?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTJhZDlmOC1jZTk2LWU5MTEtYWJjNC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_XD_RSO-i3MoYHk5mzZ4FbmPdbV40uDKMzAXCUquiG0=)

Dan

name: Cory Booker
subject: I want to hear directly from you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Team, In just two days, I'll be taking the stage for the first 2020 Democratic Presidential Debate -- thanks to people like you who believed in me and in this campaign. From the very beginning, we've been dedicated to building something that's about so much more than just one person. That's why, as part of my debate prep, I want to hear directly from the people who got me to this point. [**_So before Wednesday, I want to know: What do you want to hear from me in the first debate?_**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b909b/1788392529/VEsH/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/cory-wants-to-hear-from-you?source=em_20190624") Over the past couple of weeks, my team and I have been working to find ways to make the most of my time on the stage. Everyone knows this is a crowded race. (They even had to split the debate into two nights!) That's why it's crucial for an underdog like me to make sure I'm communicating clearly and effectively every second I get -- and this is how you can help with that. **[**_Take this one-question survey, and let me know what 's important for you to see in this debate._**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b909b/1788392529/VEsE/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/cory-wants-to-hear-from-you?source=em_20190624")** Hope to make you proud up there. Thanks, Cory [](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b909b/1788392529/VEsF/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/cory-wants-to-hear-from-you?source=em_20190624")

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b9099/1788392529/VEsD/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b9098/1788392529/VEsA/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b90e7/1788392529/VEsB/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc09b5/5fca0d19/6e8fbcc/291b90e6/1788392529/VEsO/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge: Julian must reach 130,000 donors this summer to secure his spot on the Democratic debate stage. Julian hasn't qualified yet, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money. Will you help him get there? "" Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know "". If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe "", and we'll make sure to process your request.

name: David Wysong, Beto for America
subject: This poll from Beto’s city council race in 2005
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
I’ve known Beto for close to 20 years. We go way back to the days when Beto was just starting to think about running for El Paso City Council. Coincidentally, we first met while talking to a mutual friend about running for school board!
#
I’ve known Beto for close to 20 years. We go way back to the days when Beto was just starting to think about running for El Paso City Council. Coincidentally, we first met while talking to a mutual friend about running for school board!
Beto was part of a group of ambitious young people that wanted to improve El Paso and decided to run for office in order to make it happen. It’s safe to say we knew very little about how to run a race, what you were -- or weren’t -- supposed to say, who you were supposed to spend time talking to. So Beto just got after it -- knocking doors, saying what he thought no matter the political consequences, and spending time with everyday people at town hall meetings, restaurants, parks, on busses, anywhere we could find people.
There was an initial poll that showed Beto losing by 20 points against the Mayor pro-tem, Anthony Cobos. Nobody thought we’d win against such a popular incumbent. But we worked like crazy and met with, and listened to, as many people as we could. Lots of volunteers (me included), lots of doors, plenty of sunscreen and way too many rice krispy treats (not sure why that stuck with me). It was also during that race when Beto met, started dating, and became engaged to Amy.
**Come election time, Beto won by 14 points!**

A poll shows us losing by 10 just before the election.
A few years passed and I had gone back to my normal job and life. As I followed along in the community, I saw the new progressives on city council push for a more ambitious city that would attract and retain its young people. They pushed for health benefits for same sex partners and a discussion over ending the prohibition of marijuana. Those stances brought controversy and friction as they were certainly...let’s see how to put this...ahead of their time.
That is when Beto embarked on the next underdog campaign. For U.S. Congress. Against a 16-year incumbent. Yet again, people said, “Beto can’t win. Silvestre Reyes is unbeatable. He has been the head of the House Intelligence Committee and will have the backing of everyone.”
Well, they were partially right. He did have three times the money, did have support from the big PACs, and did have tons of endorsements including a visit from Bill Clinton to El Paso where they rallied at the Coliseum.
The initial El Paso Times poll showed us getting clobbered by seven points, and our opponent had taken to running attack ads criticizing Beto for supporting legalizing marijuana. Most everyone thought we were toast... except for the amazing ALL-VOLUNTEER campaign.
**And then Beto won by six points in one of the biggest upsets of the year. In a field of five. Without a runoff.**

Beto and I visit DC after winning the congressional campaign.
I moved out to Washington to serve as Beto’s chief of staff in the House of Representatives.
When Trump won in 2016, Beto and Amy felt like they had to do something, and so they did. I admittedly was less enthusiastic.
This is probably where many of you join the story...
**Beto filed to run against Ted Cruz, the runner-up for the 2016 Republican presidential nomination.**
The other senator from Texas, John Cornyn, said, “I know Beto. And he’s a good guy. But I think this is a suicide mission... We haven’t elected a Democrat statewide since ...1994.” In 2016, Trump won the state by nine. In the last Senate race, the Republican won by 27 points.
**Against all odds, we knocked on more doors than any campaign in the state’s history. We raised a record-breaking amount in grassroots donations. And we earned more votes statewide than any Democrat in Texas history.**
**The very last poll showed us down 9 points. We came within 2.6%.**
For as long as I’ve known Beto, people have counted him out.
They’re doing the same thing now. But Beto always has a way of beating the odds — connecting with people on a human level, showing what honest leadership looks like, and purely outworking every other candidate in the running.
The first debate is in just a couple days, and our FEC fundraising deadline is June 30th.
I know Beto can win. We’ve seen him overcome the odds before. But he can’t do it alone. And we never have. Every campaign we’ve ever won (and the historic Senate race we just ran) was built on top of an outpouring of grassroots support — everyday people pitching in what they could. So today I’m asking for your help.
[Can you chip in $3 before our big debate and critical FEC deadline at the end of the month? With your support, I know we can elect Beto as our next president.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17951?refcode=ema20190624-38514&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=38514%2E444211%2EQA_zFR)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17951?refcode=ema20190624-38514&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=38514%2E444211%2EQA_zFR)**
If there’s one thing I’ve learned over the years, it’s that you should _never_ count Beto out.
David Wysong
Beto for America

But we worked like crazy and met with, and listened to, as many people as we could. Lots of volunteers (me included), lots of doors, plenty of sunscreen and way too many rice krispy treats (not sure why that stuck with me). Elizabeth's plans are informed by the conversations she has on the campaign trail. She wouldn't have time for all of those conversations if she devoted most of her schedule to chatting up big donors. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e204/45c30650/16752c788/669a55f2/1346770719/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")

The next FEC fundraising deadline is just six days away.

On a typical campaign right now, the candidate would be sprinting from big city to big city and from swanky fundraiser to swanky fundraiser.

They'd spend their time scooping up big checks from wealthy donors behind closed doors.

But that's not Elizabeth's style. This week, she'll host public town halls to take questions from voters in Miami and Chicago (before and after she's in Wednesday night's Democratic presidential primary debate).

**Elizabeth has time to run a truly grassroots campaign because she doesn 't stuff her schedule with any exclusive fundraising events for big donors.

body:
As John prepares for Thursday's first presidential debate, I'm thinking about this: In the first Democratic debate of the 2016 cycle, over 15 million folks tuned in. And 30 million people eventually v | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377178c/691c9bd/6dad582a/11a43d51/2843908683/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend: As the campaign's Communications Director, I think all day about how to get John's message out to as many people as we possibly can.

As John prepares for Thursday's first presidential debate, I'm thinking about this: In the first Democratic debate of the 2016 cycle, over 15 million folks tuned in--roughly half of the 30 million people who eventually voted in the 2016 Democratic primary.

**If that pattern holds, over half the votes cast in next year 's primary could be influenced--even decided--this week. A contribution now will help us make the most of this critical moment: Can you chip in today?** [](https://secure.hickenlooper. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130k-new-donor?refcode=em_fr_20190624_LH_20190624-fr-LH-15million-ndnr_ndnr_contribbutton1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Before John takes the stage on Thursday, we need to build up the resources to reach the millions of Americans who'll be tuning in to this race for the first time, and who'll wake up on Friday morning wanting to know a lot more about John and his progressive, unifying vision for our country.

**We need to be ready to get them up to speed:** by running ads, hiring organizers, and funding some of the widest, most ambitious voter outreach of this primary.

**We 're still over $80,000 from hitting the goal we've set for our Miami Debate Fund. name: Brandon Beach
subject: FW: Bold Campaign Strategy
body:
Friend,

I wanted to share this confidential message about our campaign strategy with you. After reading through it you will see how our bold ideas will help us win this campaign and deliver real results for the people of Georgia.

My campaign wouldn’t be anything without the support of our friends, family, and community. Please consider donating to our fight to take back the sixth: http://www.beachforcongress.com/Donate.

-B

Sent from iPhone

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Heath Garrett <heath@beachforcongress.com>
Date: Sun, Jun 23, 2019
Subject: Bold Campaign Strategy
To: <bbeach@beachforcongress.com>

Brandon –

With the Democrats pushing a radical, socialistic agenda that in no way resonates with the people of the 6th District, voters need leaders like you who are serious, willing to make the tough calls, and offer bold ideas even in the face of criticism.

I think it’s time we start letting voters know about your Be Bold message.

Whether as a candidate or as an officeholder, you’ve shown that you’re a resilient fighter who won’t give up on his principles and will do what it takes to win the primary and defeat Lucy McBath. Your Be Bold message is aready connecting with voters. We just need to let more of them know about it.

Moving forward, our effort should highlight what makes you the most qualified to lead our district:

~ Delivered the largest income tax cut in state history
~ Balanced the budget each year and secured a AAA bond rating
~ Led the fight for the largest transportation investment in state history
~ Fully-funded public schools, raised teacher salaries, and increased options for parents
~ Created a true culture of life in our state
~ Improved public safety by creating an anti-gang task force
~ Authorized a pull out of all Obamacare mandates
~ A-rated by the NRA and a staunch defender of 2nd Amendment

This is going to be a long and hard-fought campaign, but unlike our opponents, you don’t just talk about conservative solutions, you deliver on them. That’s what voters want.

It’s imperative we have a strong end of quarter fundraising number to show Lucy McBath that she’ll need to actually come to Georgia and campaign if she wants to win next year. Lets let our key people know we need their help before next Sunday.

Heath

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you are no longer interested you can unsubscribe instantly:
https://brandonbeach.cmail20.com/t/r-u-jidiyddd-udyuidthb-r/

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump sanctions the worst elements of the Iranian regime
body:
The President’s actions are a strong yet proportionate response to Iran’s recent string of provocative behaviors. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b90826d3f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • June 24, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump sanctions the worst elements of the Iranian regime**

**President Donald J. Trump** signed an executive order this morning that imposes tough sanctions to deprive the Supreme Leader of Iran and some of his closest associates of key financial resources.
**🎬 Watch:**[President Trump announces new sanctions on Iranian leaders](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f6e8d91b87&e=3ee4985eea)

**“Today’s** **action** follows a series of aggressive behaviors by the Iranian regime in recent weeks,” the President said from the Oval Office. “The Supreme Leader of Iran is [the] one who ultimately is responsible for the hostile conduct of the regime . . . His office oversees the regime’s most brutal instruments, including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.”

**The President’s actions** are a strong yet proportionate response to Iran’s recent string of provocative behaviors. Last week, Iran shot down an American drone over international waters. Earliest this month, Iran attacked two commercial vessels and used proxy forces to attack civilian infrastructure in Saudi Arabia.

**Perhaps most alarming** , the regime also recently announced that it will be increasing its stockpile of enriched uranium in the coming days.

**Today’s sanctions** send a firm message to Tehran: They’re no longer dealing with an Administration naive or indifferent to their malign behavior. In [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b1f5d425da&e=3ee4985eea), Middle East expert Tom Basile called former President Obama’s “sweetheart deal with Iran” his worst foreign policy blunder of all.

**“Obama** **wanted** his Iran deal at all costs, and those costs were steep. It amounted to access to more than $100 billion, ending most sanctions, opening the country to foreign investment, among other benefits for the regime,” Basile writes. In other words, the Iran deal handed the world’s largest state sponsor of terror unprecedented financial capabilities.

**Those** **days are over.** Last year, President Trump withdrew the United States from the deal and put the regime on notice—either change course or face crippling economic pressure. That choice became even clearer today when the President authorized putting sanctions on anyone who provides material support to the Supreme Leader’s Office.

[America is standing firm against the worst elements of the Iranian regime.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eacd0c1af1&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch:** [Iran can never get a nuclear weapon, President Trump says](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7add2fe576&e=3ee4985eea)

---
|
---
|
# **What American patients have a right to know—before they go to the doctor**

**President Trump’s mission** for healthcare can be summed up in just four words: put American patients first.

**Today, he took** another step toward that goal, signing an executive order that requires hospitals to [publicly disclose what people actually pay](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc67b33fce&e=3ee4985eea) for important medical services in an easy-to-read format.

**For too long** , powerful interests have blocked patients from knowing the true price and quality of their healthcare, preventing them from making informed choices. Lack of transparency contributes to soaring costs, suppressed competition, and lower quality of care. As a result, patients face wild variations in prices—often for the exact same services.

**Publicizing the _true_ prices** of healthcare services will allow Americans to make choices that fit their personal healthcare needs and their financial situations. “We believe the American people have the right to know the price of services before they go to visit the doctor,” President Trump said today.

[Watch President Trump unveil today's landmark healthcare action](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0ad31361d1&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

Trump signed an executive order this morning that imposes tough sanctions to deprive the Supreme Leader of Iran and some of his closest associates of key financial resources. 🎬 Watch: “Today’s action follows a series of aggressive behaviors by the Iranian regime in recent weeks,” the President said from the Oval Office. “The Supreme Leader of Iran is [the] one who ultimately is responsible for the hostile conduct of the regime . . . His office oversees the regime’s most brutal instruments, including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.” The President’s actions are a strong yet proportionate response to Iran’s recent string of provocative behaviors. Last week, Iran shot down an American drone over international waters. Earliest this month, Iran attacked two commercial vessels and used proxy forces to attack civilian infrastructure in Saudi Arabia. Perhaps most alarming , the regime also recently announced that it will be increasing its stockpile of enriched uranium in the coming days. Today’s sanctions send a firm message to Tehran: They’re no longer dealing with an Administration naive or indifferent to their malign behavior. In , Middle East expert Tom Basile called former President Obama’s “sweetheart deal with Iran” his worst foreign policy blunder of all. “Obama wanted his Iran deal at all costs, and those costs were steep. It amounted to access to more than $100 billion, ending most sanctions, opening the country to foreign investment, among other benefits for the regime,” Basile writes. In other words, the Iran deal handed the world’s largest state sponsor of terror unprecedented financial capabilities. Those days are over. Last year, President Trump withdrew the United States from the deal and put the regime on notice—either change course or face crippling economic pressure. That choice became even clearer today when the President authorized putting sanctions on anyone who provides material support to the Supreme Leader’s Office. Watch: | | What American patients have a right to know—before they go to the doctor President Trump’s mission for healthcare can be summed up in just four words: put American patients first. Today, he took another step toward that goal, signing an executive order that requires hospitals to for important medical services in an easy-to-read format. For too long , powerful interests have blocked patients from knowing the true price and quality of their healthcare, preventing them from making informed choices. Lack of transparency contributes to soaring costs, suppressed competition, and lower quality of care. As a result, patients face wild variations in prices—often for the exact same services. Publicizing the true prices of healthcare services will allow Americans to make choices that fit their personal healthcare needs and their financial situations. “We believe the American people have the right to know the price of services before they go to visit the doctor,” President Trump said today. . | | Photo of the Day | Official White House Photo by Joyce N.
